mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) above 0.5% in a month signals central bank tightening, increasing discount rates and reducing equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite rising CPIAUCSL.

mechanism_narrative: A unemployment rate (UNRATE) above 6.5% signals weak labor market conditions, reducing consumer spending and corporate earnings, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite UNRATE > 6.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in industrial production (INDPRO) and declining federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS) indicates accommodative monetary policy supporting supply-side expansion, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: Rank IC for combined INDPRO/FEDFUNDS signals drops below 0.05 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: In late-cycle expansions, rising housing starts (HOUST) signal overbuilding and potential demand saturation, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 50% of late-cycle periods despite rising HOUST.

mechanism_narrative: Strong real sales (RSAFS) above 550,000 million dollars indicate robust consumer demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 40% of triggered instances despite RSAFS > 550,000.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in payrolls (PAYEMS) and declining unemployment (UNRATE) indicates a strong labor market, boosting corporate earnings and driving positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC for combined PAYEMS/UNRATE signals drops below 0.03 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) indicates expanding supply capacity, supporting corporate profits and driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 40% of INDPRO growth periods.
